$comment: D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER (writable data sections) RUNTIME witness: a mutable initialized global (gd=5 -> .data) and a tentative zero-init global (gz -> .bss) are each WRITTEN at runtime (gd=gd+2; gz=35) then READ back into the exit code (7 + 35 = 42). Before this cycle the store to gd faulted (SIGSEGV / exit 139) because asm.cpp routed every initialized global to read-only .rodata; now mutable -> writable .data, tentative -> writable .bss (zero-fill). The reads are real runtime global LOADs (a store-then-reload the optimizer cannot fold away), so the stores genuinely execute on every target. The release optimizedPipelines arm proves optimized==baseline. PE runs on windows; ELF on linux; arm64-ELF under qemu-aarch64; arm64-macho on the macos-latest (Apple Silicon) CI leg.
